rulings.Case Title: GCFS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. HAMMOND, GERALD I AKA JERRY CLIFFORD HAMMOND et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, May 14, 2025, Line 1. ASSIGNEE ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS INVESTMENTS, LLC HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. On calendar is a Hearing on Claim of Exemption. Hearing required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: STELLA CRUZ ET AL VS. COM HOWARD I LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, May 14, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANTs COM HOWARD I LLC, NEW CIVIC COMPANY, LTD, and DAVIDSON HOTEL COMPANY LLC.'s Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant Com Howard 1 LLC, New Civic Company, Ltd. and Davidson Hotel Company LLC's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, May 14, 2025, Line 4. DEFENDANT WISH US HOLDINGS LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Wish US Holdings LLC's demurrer is OFF CALENDAR. On February 13, 2025, the court took Defendant's earlier demurrer off calendar and granted Plaintiff Genoa Martell leave to file a second amended complaint on or before March 7, 2025. Importantly, at that time the court ordered that subsequent meet and confer regarding demurrers must be by "remote platform such as Zoom" and "recorded." This directive was to avoid the incessant finger pointing that has come to characterize this case. For this demurrer, Defense counsel testifies the meet and confer occurred during "a phone call conversation." (Wahl Dec. para. 5. ) Defendant failed to follow the court's order and, therefore, the demurrer is off calendar. In its directive, the court contemplated that the next demurrer would be to the second amended complaint, but that never got filed. The court's intent, though, was clear: subsequent Code-required meet and confer must be by remote video platform such as Zoom and recorded by the parties. On its own motion, the court grants Plaintiff leave to file her second amended complaint no later than May 23, 2025. No extensions of time as to this order are contemplated. All Code-required meet and confer must be conducted by remote video platform such as Zoom and recorded by the parties. Going forward, the parties are ordered to make a concerted effort to cooperate to bring this case to resolution. "It is the policy of the state that a plaintiff shall proceed with reasonable diligence in the prosecution of an action but that all parties shall cooperate in bringing the action to trial or other disposition." (Code of Civil Procedure section 583.130.) "The policy of the state is that the parties to a lawsuit 'shall cooperate.' Period. Full stop." (Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 130.) Defendant shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order setting out the above text verbatim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: EMA BELL VS. WHOLE FOODS MARKET SERVICES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, May 14, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF EMA BELL's Motion To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Ema Bell's unopposed Motion To File Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the court grants Plaintiff leave to file the proposed Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff must file her Second Amended Complaint, which must be substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Stipulation, within ten days of entry of this order.
